Sources.Notes: Official HubSpot developer documentation hub. Covers CRM, Conversations, Automation/Workflow, Calling Extensions, CMS, Marketing. Versioned URLs (/api-reference/latest/ and dated betas like /api-reference/2026-09-beta/) indicate active maintenance. HTML — parseable but not as machine-friendly as OpenAPI.
Entry-point overview for HubSpot's public APIs. Best source for auth model (private apps, OAuth, scopes) and rate-limit conventions. Pair with /docs for object-level surface.
Officially advertised on /docs as the canonical documentation index for LLM/agent consumption. Best entry point for programmatic crawling — use this to enumerate every reference page before scraping.

HubSpot-owned repo containing OpenAPI 3.0 schemas for the public APIs (PublicApiSpecs/ folder). README states the specs are 'intended solely for HubSpot's internal use, specifically for our Postman integration' — so format may shift without notice, but they ARE the source the Postman workspace consumes. Best machine-readable surface for code generation. Authority docked to 4 because of the internal-use disclaimer.

Official HubSpot Node.js SDK (V3 APIs). 100% TypeScript, generated from HubSpot's internal OpenAPI specs via the OpenAPI code generator. Latest release v13.5.0 (March 2026). npm: @hubspot/api-client. Excellent reference for object shapes, auth, pagination, retry conventions — and a candidate transport layer.
Catalog of HubSpot's official client SDKs (Node.js, Python, PHP, Ruby). Confirms which language SDKs are first-party. Use to cross-check SDK behavior parity.

Official HubSpot Postman workspace, linked from developers.hubspot.com/postman. Collections cover CRM, CMS, Marketing, etc., kept in sync with the spec collection repo. Good cross-check for endpoint shapes and example payloads. WebFetch returned minimal content (likely JS-rendered) — verified via the official /postman landing page.

Official HubSpot landing page that authoritatively links to the Postman workspace plus the specialized Flows workspace (https://www.postman.com/hubspot/workspace/hubspot-flows-workspace). Use to establish authority of the Postman collections.

Changelog post announcing the api-catalog-public endpoint. NOTE: the advertised endpoint (https://api.hubspot.com/api-catalog-public/v1/apis) currently 404s — community threads confirm intermittent availability. Documented here only as evidence that an official catalog endpoint is intended to exist; do NOT rely on it as a live source.

GapsIdentified: Official OpenAPI specs are published in a repo HubSpot labels 'internal use only' — format/structure may shift without deprecation notices.
The api-catalog-public/v1/apis endpoint advertised in HubSpot's changelog currently returns 404; cannot be used as a runtime spec discovery source.
HubSpot does not announce changes to OpenAPI specs in the changelog — drift detection must be done by diffing the spec-collection repo on a schedule.

Authentication flows (private apps, OAuth 2.0 install, scopes) are documented in HTML but not present in the OpenAPI specs (specs cover endpoints, not the auth dance) — requires scraping /docs/getting-started/.
Rate-limit and quota behavior is documented separately from endpoint specs; will need a dedicated extraction pass.
Custom-objects (user-defined schemas) require runtime discovery — not present in the static OpenAPI surface.
AuthClassification: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code grant with refresh tokens; alternative private-app static access tokens supported. Wire-form: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>. Maps to MJ CredentialTypeID='APIKey' for private apps OR 'OAuth2AuthCode' for OAuth install.
PaginationClassification: Cursor (paging.next.after response path; ?after=<cursor> request parameter; ?limit=<n> page size).
